message.reasoning_content:
We need to fill the stubs with full CandidateRecord for Hivel1 through Hivel8. However the roster_stubs only provide first 8. But the prompt says: "Return a JSON object {"candidates": [...]} containing one COMPLETE CandidateRecord per roster stub above, in the same order, with identical record_id values." So we need to create full records for Hivel1 to Hivel8. Wait, the roster_stubs array has 8 entries. But the extraction skeleton had many; the roster only includes first 8. So we generate 8 candidates. I'll read the provided scaffold, paper text, and tables to fill them. I need to comply with the schema and rules.

I see the scaffold has incomplete? Actually they provided a partial scaffold with method_chain etc. They want me to output only the candidates array with full records for the stubs. The roster includes Hivel1 through Hivel8. So I will fill those.

Key points:
- For each candidate, we need inclusion_assessment, candidate_origin, core (observed_phase_space, derived_kinematics, bound_assessment), photometry, spectroscopy, stellar_parameters, abundances, quality_flags, orbit, astrophysical_origin, extra.
- I need to extract values from ECSV tables (table-001, table-002, table-006, table-007, table-008) and paper text.
- The candidate_origin: All these are new discoveries in this paper, so introduced_by_this_paper. The paper does not cite previous literature for these 20 fastest, except that only 14 are in OFSC, but these 20 are all new? Actually the paper says only 14 of the 591 were reported in OFSC. So many are new. For these 20 fastest, I'll assume they are introduced by this paper unless I find a citation. I'll check: The text says "only 14 of them were reported in the current version of the open fast stars catalog (OFSC)". But that's for the 591 overall. No specific mention that any of the 20 fastest were previously reported. So I'll treat them as introduced_by_this_paper.
- Method chain is already defined; I'll use existing step ids: step-02 for cross-match (input catalog), step-03 for selection, step-04 for radial velocity, step-05 solar params, step-06 distance estimation, step-07 velocity calculation, step-08 potential models, step-09 escape assessment, step-10 orbit integration, step-11 classification, step-12 stellar parameters, step-13 quality filter. I need to set method_refs accordingly.
- For RA/Dec: from table-001, columns col_002 (R.A. deg), col_003 (decl. deg). Values are decimal degrees. Frame? The paper states positions are from Gaia DR2. According to coordinate_frames.md, Gaia DR2 has ICRS at reference epoch J2015.5. I'll set reference_frame.value = "ICRS", epoch.value = "J2015.5", epoch_kind = "reference_epoch", inference_basis = "survey_reference". Also include source_refs from paper and ECSV.
- For photometry: G, G_BP, G_RP from table-001, cols 010, 011, 012.
- For spectroscopy: The paper provides spectral type Class (table-001 col_005), and RV_LASP (col_006) is already in observed_phase_space. I'll put Class as spectroscopy with measurement_type "spectral_type", and RV as radial_velocity. No separate RV follow-up? I'll just use the RV in observed_phase_space. The paper mentions spectral S/N (col_004), I can put that as signal_to_noise. I'll include a spectroscopy record for spectral type and another for S/N.
- Stellar parameters from table-008 (LASP and DD-Payne). I'll pick Teff, log g, [Fe/H] from LASP (cols 002-004) as "stellar_parameters". Maybe also include DD-Payne values as extra or additional? The schema has stellar_parameters.teff, log_g, metallicity. I'll use LASP values. DD-Payne can go in extra or maybe as separate stellar parameters? But schema only one stellar_parameters object with optional other array. I can put LASP values in the standard teff, log_g, metallicity, and DD-Payne values as extra quantity records with appropriate names, like "Teff_DD-Payne", "log_g_DD-Payne", "[Fe/H]_DD-Payne", "[alpha/Fe]_DD-Payne". That matches the paper's naming.
- Abundances: [alpha/Fe] from DD-Payne. I'll put that as abundance record with element "alpha", abundance_scale "dex", reference_element "Fe".
- Quality_flags: astrometric_flag from table-001 col_013. That's a flag (0 or 1) indicating high-quality astrometry. I'll include a quality flag record with name "astrometric_flag", value as the flag, method_refs step-13.
- Orbit: from table-002 (e, Zmax, r_min, E-Phi(infinity)). For Hivel1: e = "-" (not provided), Zmax = "-", r_min = "13.7 ...", E-Phi = "0.0033 ...". But the paper says e and Zmax are not provided for unbound stars (they say only provide for bound stars with Zmax <= 200 kpc). So Hivel1 is unbound (P_ub > 0.5 in all models), so e and Zmax are not given. I'll include them as null or leave absent? Schema allows null; I'll set null. r_min and E-Phi are given. I'll use those.
- Astrophysical_origin: They have Origin_Class from table-007, which is classification per step-11. I'll include origin_classification and maybe origin_site. Also P_gc, P_MW are probabilities from orbit, and P_ub from escape. I'll put P_gc and P_MW as hypothesis_metrics? Actually the schema has astrophysical_origin.hypothesis_metrics[], where each metric has hypothesis (like "Galactic Center ejection", "Galactic disk ejection", "unbound") with metric_type (probability). Also P_ub per model can be multiple entries. I'll include for Hivel1: P_gc, P_MW, and P_ub (W) etc. But bound_assessment already has unbound_probability for each model perhaps? The bound_assessment has unbound_probability as a single quantity record; but the paper has multiple models. I think I'll put the bound_assessment.unbound_probability for the Watkins+2019 model (the most conservative) as the main one, and maybe note all in extra or in hypothesis_metrics. Actually the schema's bound_assessment expects a single unbound_probability? It's a nullable QuantityRecord. So I could put the one from Watkins+2019. Then in hypothesis_metrics I can list all model probabilities. I'll do that.

Now, I'll construct the JSON with all required fields. I need to ensure all source_refs are correct, method_refs match the steps. I'll use the ECSV cells and paper lines.

I'll fill Hivel1 as example and then repeat for others. Hivel1: 
RA: 240.3374980 deg, Dec: 41.16681800 deg. From table-001, line 43, columns col_002 and col_003. I'll create CoordinateQuantityRecord with decimal_degrees, frame ICRS, epoch J2015.5, inference_basis survey_reference, source_refs to the ECSV cell and also maybe paper text (the table header says (deg)). 
RV: -179 +/- 5 km/s from col_006. Method: radial_velocity_measurement (step-04) because it's LASP corrected.
Parallax: 0.118 +/- 0.016 mas, col_009.
Proper motion ra: -25.759 +/- 0.025 mas/yr, col_007.
Proper motion dec: -9.745 +/- 0.040 mas/yr, col_008.
Distance: not directly given in table-001, but table-002 gives r_GC and x,y,z. But distance is not directly provided in observed_phase_space. The schema has distance as optional. I can leave distance null because they don't give heliocentric distance; they compute r_GC. I could include r_GC in derived_kinematics.galactocentric_radius. So distance stays null.
Derived kinematics: galactocentric_x, y, z from table-002; galactocentric_vx, vy, vz; total_velocity V_GC; maybe galactocentric_tangential_velocity not given. I'll include galactocentric_x, y, z, galactocentric_radius, galactocentric_vx, vy, vz, total_velocity, tangential_velocity null, galactocentric_tangential_velocity null, galactic_rest_frame_velocity same as V_GC. Note: The paper's V_GC is Galactocentric total velocity, which is the velocity in the Galactic rest frame. So I'll set total_velocity = V_GC, and galactic_rest_frame_velocity = V_GC. Actually the schema has both: total_velocity and galactic_rest_frame_velocity; I'll set both to same value? Possibly. I'll include total_velocity.
Bound assessment: escape_velocity for each model; I'll pick Vesc(W) as the main escape_velocity, and maybe include all in extra or in escaped velocity ratio? The escape_velocity ratio could be V_GC/Vesc(W). I'll compute: V_GC = 922, Vesc(W)=598, ratio = 1.54 approx. I can compute and put value with method_ref step-09. I'll compute ratio as 922/598 = 1.542... I'll put raw_value maybe from table-006: no ratio given. I can compute as plain numbers. I'll provide ratio in escope_velocity_ratio. Also escape_margin maybe V_GC - Vesc(W). unbound_probability from table-007 Pub(W) = 0.996. bound_probability 1 - 0.996 = 0.004? No, unbound_probability is Pub. I'll set bound_probability null, unbound_probability value 0.996, raw_value "0.996". bound_status_metric maybe the energy difference E-Phi which I can put as a metric.
Photometry: G=13.01, G_BP=13.00, G_RP=15.21. From table-001, all Gaia magnitude, so measurement_type "magnitude", survey "Gaia", band G, G_BP, G_RP. I'll include uncertainties? They are not given, so error empty.
Spectroscopy: class "G7" from col_005, measurement_type "spectral_type", spectral_type "G7". S/N_r = 158 from col_004, measurement_type "signal_to_noise", no line. RV from col_006 would be in observed_phase_space, but I could also note that it's LASP RV. I'll include a spectroscopy record for RV measurement? Not needed; already in observed.
Stellar parameters: from table-008 LASP: Teff=4803 +/- 19, log_g=1.89 +/- 0.03, [Fe/H] = -1.40 +/- 0.02. I'll put these as teff, log_g (unit "dex"), metallicity (unit "dex"). Also extra for DD-Payne: Teff_DD-Payne=4919 +/- 38, log_g_DD-Payne=37.62 +/- 2.22 (note units: log g is in dex but the value 37.62 seems erroneous? Possibly a typo in paper? Actually table-008 shows "37.62 +/- 2.22" for log g, which is unrealistic. I'll still record raw_value as is. Then [Fe/H]_DD-Payne = -1.25 +/- 0.06, [alpha/Fe] = 0.28 +/- 0.02.
Abundances: [alpha/Fe] abundance element "alpha", abundance_scale "dex", reference_element "Fe". Value 0.28, raw_value "0.28 +/- 0.02". I'll include error.
Quality flag: astrometric_flag = 0. So quality flag name "astrometric_flag", value "0". Note that 0 means not conservative high quality.
Orbit: from table-002: eccentricity e = "-" (not provided), so I'll set null. Zmax = "-", null. r_min = "13.7_{-1.4}^{+1.9}". So I'll give raw_value, value = 13.7, lower_error 1.4, upper_error 1.9, unit kpc. E-Phi = "0.0033_{-0.0013}^{+0.0019}" unit kpc^2 Myr^-2. I'll put as named quantity in orbit.other? The schema has specific fields: eccentricity, pericenter, apocenter, zmax, flight_time, disk_crossing_radius, angular_momentum, other. So r_min is disk_crossing_radius. And E-Phi is not standard; I'll put in other.
Astrophysical origin: Origin_Class "HRS". So origin_classification value "HRS". Origin_site maybe from P_gc, P_MW: P_gc=0.000, P_MW=1.000. So not GC, likely from disk. I can set origin_site to "Galactic disk" but need source. I'll put a QuantityRecord with value "Galactic disk" maybe? Not numeric. Might leave null and use hypothesis_metrics. I'll include hypothesis_metrics for P_gc, P_MW, P_ub per model.
Now, I need to include all those probabilities. I'll use hypothesis_metrics array for: P_gc (hypothesis "Galactic Center ejection", metric_type "probability", value 0.0), P_MW (hypothesis "Galactic disk ejection", probability, 1.0), and P_ub for each model. Also origin_classification is a QuantityRecord, so I'll set raw_value "HRS", value "HRS", source from table-007 col_011. Method step-11.

Now I'll build Hivel1 candidate. Then similar for others, adjusting numbers from tables.

Now, I must ensure that method_refs are exactly one direct producer. I'll map: RA/Dec -> step-02 (input_catalog) and step-02 is the cross-match; actually they come from Gaia DR2, so input_catalog. So method_refs: ["step-02"]. Parallax, proper motions -> also Gaia DR2, so step-02. Radial velocity -> step-04 (radial_velocity_measurement). Distance is derived from parallax, so method_refs: ["step-06"]. Galactocentric positions -> step-06 (distance_estimation). Galactocentric velocities (Vx, Vy, Vz, total) -> step-07 (velocity_calculation). Escape velocities -> step-09 (escape_or_bound_assessment). Unbound probabilities -> step-09. Orbit e, Zmax, r_min, E-Phi -> step-10 (orbit_integration). P_gc, P_MW -> step-10. Origin class -> step-11 (candidate_classification). Astrometric_flag -> step-13 (quality_filter). Photometry G magnitudes -> step-02 (Gaia DR2). Spectral type, S/N -> step-02 (from LAMOST), but step-02 is cross-match, not per se. I think method_refs for LAMOST parameters should be step-02 (input_catalog) as the cross-match catalog contains LAMOST data. But the step-02 is cross_match; it's the LAMOST-Gaia catalog. I'll use step-02 for those originally from those surveys. So spectral type from LAMOST 1D pipeline (provided in the LAMOST-Gaia catalog) is step-02. Stellar parameters LASP are also from LAMOST-Gaia, so step-02. DD-Payne parameters are from step-12 (stellar_parameter_inference). I'll assign accordingly.

For Hivel1, astrometric_flag=0, so quality flag method step-13.

Now, inclusion_assessment: The paper labels these as "HiVelSCs" (high velocity star candidates), and some are classified as HRS, HVS, etc. For Hivel1, origin_class "HRS", meaning hyper-runaway star. The paper says that HiVelSCs include HRS, RS, OUT, HVS. They define HRS as hyper-runaway star with P_ub > 0.5 and P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5. HRS are unbound from Galaxy. So Hivel1 is unbound. Paper label: "HRS candidate" (from Origin_Class). Also "HiVelSC" and "high velocity star candidate". I'll set paper_labels: ["high_velocity_star", "hyper_runaway_candidate"] (maybe "hvs_candidate"? Actually HRS is hyper-runaway candidate. I'll use "hyper_runaway_candidate", and also "high_velocity_star".) The galactic_bound_claim: "unbound" because P_ub > 0.5 in Watkins+2019 (the most conservative) say at least 43 are unbound with >50%, and Hivel1 is one of them (P_ub(W)=0.996). So claim is "unbound". inclusion_basis: "explicit_candidate_text" because the paper explicitly lists them as HiVelSCs and classifies them. extraction_confidence: high. source_refs from paper text where they mention the 20 fastest and that they are unbound probabilities, and the table. I'll cite lines 478-485 (description of unbound probabilities), and the table captions.

candidate_origin: introduced_by_this_paper true. Origin_type: "introduced_by_this_paper". No citation.

Now, I'll assemble for Hivel1. Then repeat pattern for Hivel2-Hivel8.

I'll need to be careful with coordinate epoch: Gaia DR2 has reference epoch J2015.5, but the paper in Section 2.2 uses the same Gaia astrometry with correlation matrix. So I'll set epoch.value "J2015.5", epoch_kind "reference_epoch". The Gaia DR2 positions are at reference epoch J2015.5, not equinox. So I'll use that.

Now I'll produce the JSON.
